note: Pathologic is a single text-format filter plugin (filter_pathologic) with a global config object and per-format overrides. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want Pathologic to correct URLs in content of a text format. Which filter do I enable, and where in the filter processing order should it go?
evals.expected_output: Enable the 'Correct URLs with Pathologic' filter (plugin id filter_pathologic) on the text format and place it last in the filter processing order.

evals.prompt: Which Pathologic setting chooses between full absolute URLs, protocol-relative URLs, and root-relative paths, and what are its three values?
evals.expected_output: The protocol_style setting: 'full' (http://example.com/foo/bar), 'proto-rel' (//example.com/foo/bar), and 'path' (/foo/bar relative to server root). Default is full.

evals.prompt: My Drupal site is served over both HTTP and HTTPS and Pathologic's full absolute URLs cause mixed-protocol problems. Which protocol_style value avoids that while still producing host-qualified URLs?
evals.expected_output: Set protocol_style to 'proto-rel' to emit protocol-relative URLs like //example.com/foo/bar, avoiding HTTP/HTTPS mixing.

evals.prompt: Content on my Drupal site was authored on a staging server at http://dev.example.org/staging/ and now has links written against that base. Which Pathologic setting do I use so those links get corrected, and how do I fill it in?
evals.expected_output: Use the local_paths setting ('All base paths for this site'): list every base path/URL the site has lived at, one per line (e.g. http://dev.example.org/staging/), so Pathologic recognizes and corrects those links.

evals.prompt: What is the config object and admin route for Pathologic's global settings on Drupal, and which permission gates the form?
evals.expected_output: Global settings live in the pathologic.settings config object, edited at /admin/config/content/pathologic (route pathologic.config_form), gated by the 'administer filters' permission.

evals.prompt: I want one text format to use different Pathologic settings than the site-wide ones. Which per-filter setting controls whether global or local settings apply?
evals.expected_output: The filter's settings_source setting: 'global' uses the site-wide pathologic.settings; 'local' uses custom per-format settings stored under local_settings.

evals.prompt: In a custom Drupal module I want to stop Pathologic from rewriting certain URLs (pass them through unchanged). Which hook do I implement and what do I set?
evals.expected_output: Implement hook_pathologic_alter(&$url_params, $parts, $settings) and set $url_params['options']['use_original'] = TRUE to return the original unaltered URL.

evals.prompt: Why does Pathologic recommend being the last filter in a text format's processing order, and what plugin weight does it ship with?
evals.expected_output: Pathologic rewrites finished markup (href/src/etc.), so it should run last after other filters; it ships with plugin weight 50 to encourage this.
